title: Vincetoxicum neglectum
intro: Vincetoxicum neglectum, also known as Lethal Knoutweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum neglectum include vincetoxin, a cardiotonic glycoside, and other cardenolides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vincetoxicum neglectum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to treat conditions such as cardiovascular disease and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum neglectum is classified in the family Apocynaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This classification places it within the order Gentianales, which consists of plants with five-merous flowers. The family Apocynaceae includes a wide range of plants, from trees to shrubs and climbing vines, with many species found in various parts of the world. The genus Vincetoxicum, to which Vincetoxicum neglectum belongs, is native to Asia and is characterized by its toxic properties and showy, fragrant flowers. Further classification of Vincetoxicum neglectum can be made at the species level, where it can be distinguished from other closely related species within the genus.
